body:
### Your current environment
The output of python collect_env.py ```text Your output of `python collect_env.py` here ```
### Your code version
The commit id or version of vllm ```text a2f713002 ```
The commit id or version of vllm-omni ```text bbc04762 ```
### 🐛 Describe the bug Reproduce bugs: `python3 -m pytest tests/entrypoints/openai_api/test_serving_speech.py` ``` Traceback: tests/entrypoints/openai_api/test_serving_speech.py:27: in from vllm_omni.entrypoints.openai import api_server as api_server_module vllm_omni/entrypoints/openai/__init__.py:12: in from vllm_omni.entrypoints.openai.api_server import ( vllm_omni/entrypoints/openai/api_server.py:68: in from vllm.entrypoints.serve.render.serving import OpenAIServingRender E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'vllm.entrypoints.serve.render.serving' ``` ### Summary After rebasing onto recent upstream vLLM, `vllm_omni/entrypoints/openai/api_server.py` is no longer aligned with upstream OpenAI serving initialization. The root cause appears to be the combination of these two upstream PRs: - https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/44285 - https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/44512 ### Root Cause PR #44285 split the old `OpenAIServingRender` / `ServingRender` responsibilities into separate renderer and entrypoint components: - `OnlineRenderer` - `OnlineDerenderer` - `ServingRender` After this change, upstream OpenAI serving no longer passes around `openai_serving_render`. Instead, it initializes: ```python state.online_renderer = OnlineRenderer(...) state.online_derenderer = OnlineDerenderer(...) ``` and passes `online_renderer=state.online_renderer` into OpenAI chat/completion/responses/tokenization serving classes. PR #44512 then moved the scale-out entrypoints from the old `serve.*` namespace into `scale_out.*`: ```python vllm.entrypoints.serve.disagg.* -> vllm.entrypoints.scale_out.token_in_token_out.* vllm.entrypoints.serve.render.* -> vllm.entrypoints.scale_out.render.* + vllm.entrypoints.scale_out.derender.* + vllm.entrypoints.scale_out.factories ``` ### Suggested Fix - using `from vllm.entrypoints.scale_out.token_in_token_out.serving import ServingTokens` - as commented in `api_server.py`, we should kept aligned with upstream `init_app_state`, replace `OpenAIServingRender` usage with `OnlineRe

comments.author: akshatvishu
comments.body: I tested this on the `main` and all test are passing: vLLM Version : 0.24.0 vLLM-Omni Version : 0.24.0rc1 (git sha: 5c51782c) So, maybe this is due to a `vllm / vllm-omni` version miss-match?

comments.author: sphinxkkkbc
comments.body: Thanks for your verification, indeed a version issue; I used the latest commit of vllm upstream > I tested this on the `main` and all test are passing: > > vLLM Version : 0.24.0 vLLM-Omni Version : 0.24.0rc1 (git sha: [5c51782](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/commit/5c51782c6bec3c7976c528e5899e89452278c448)) > > > So, maybe this is due to a `vllm / vllm-omni` version miss-match?

title: [Bug]: No module named 'vllm.entrypoints.serve.render.serving'
